text.title: Micro-MVP Cut Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that govern mechanical cutting of a feature to a day-sized slice. These prevent the most common solo-builder failure: "small" features that quietly grow to three days.

rule.statement: Cut along six axes: data model, surface, permissions, persistence, tests, UI polish. For each axis, write the minimum slice + deferred items. Reject scoping that cuts only along 1-2 axes (insufficient discipline).
rule.rationale: Cutting only along "surface" leaves data + permissions oversized. Six axes force a holistic minimum slice; the slice gets 5-10× smaller than a single-axis cut.

rule.statement: The output slice ships in ≤ 8 hours of focused work. Reject slices that exceed 8 hours OR cannot be split into a single coherent slice + deferred follow-ups.
rule.rationale: 8 hours is the practical ceiling for a single coherent build session. Past this, context switches eat the day; flow breaks; "done today" slips to "done this week."

rule.statement: Every deferred item is recorded with axis + reason in the deferral_list. Reject silent deferrals ("we'll figure it out later"). Deferrals become explicit follow-up slices in the backlog.
rule.rationale: Silent deferrals re-enter as scope creep mid-build. Explicit deferrals make the next-day choice visible and reduce mid-build temptation.

rule.statement: First slice ships with smoke tests only: one happy-path, one error-path. Reject demands for comprehensive test coverage in the first slice. Coverage expansion is a deferred slice.
rule.rationale: Comprehensive testing on the first slice doubles slice cost without proportional confidence gain. Smoke is sufficient to catch "did it boot at all?" — the actual user-validation happens next.

rule.statement: First slice ships with raw UI: default styles, no animations, no responsive polish. Polish is a deferred slice. Reject pre-launch designer review or polish hours billed to the first slice.
rule.rationale: Polish before user validation is sunk cost if the slice misses. Raw UI gets the data; polish responds to real usage signal.

example.title: Six-axis cut for a notification feature
example.code:
Feature: "User can mute a project notification"
Cut: data model: single boolean field `muted` on Project. Defer: per-channel toggles. surface: one toggle on /projects/:id page. Defer: bulk mute, settings page. permissions: owner-only. Defer: team-level mute, role-based mute. persistence: simple DB update. (Already supported; nothing to defer.) tests: smoke = "owner toggles, gets back muted=true". Defer: error paths, race tests. UI polish: default checkbox label "Mute notifications". Defer: animated toggle, copy review.
Estimate: 4 hours. Ship today. Deferred items → next-slice candidates.
example.note: Illustrates all 5 rules — 6 axes, day-sized, explicit deferrals, smoke only, raw UI.

example.title: Hidden scope creep slice
example.code:
Feature: "User can mute a project notification"
Spec: Add muted field, build settings page with per-channel toggles, add team-level mute for admins, write 8 tests, animated toggle with dark-mode variant.
Estimate: 16 hours. Ships in 2.5 days due to context switches. Deferred items: not documented.
example.note: Illustrates r1, r2, r3, r4, r5 violations — slice exceeds day, no axis discipline, no deferrals listed.
